summary: Postinstall dropper fetches and executes XOR-encrypted Windows binary from third-party GitHub
details: On `npm install`, the package's postinstall hook writes a PowerShell loader to %TEMP%\loader.ps1 that downloads launcher.bin from https://github.com/Dimitrijenco/Sticky_note/releases/download/v2/launcher.bin, XOR-decrypts it with key 0x42, writes the result to %TEMP%\tmp.exe, executes it via `Start-Process -WindowStyle Hidden`, then deletes the dropped binary. The publisher of the GitHub repository hosting the payload does not match the npm package publisher, the payload is XOR-obfuscated to evade scanning, and execution is hidden from the user. After launching the loader, the hook spawns a detached cleanup process that, after a 5-second delay, removes the `mailconfirmer` entries from the host's package.json and package-lock.json and rm -rf's its own node_modules directory — anti-forensic tampering with the installer's manifests to hide the compromise. The advertised library surface (index.js) is a stub: `sendConfirmationEmail` and `verifyToken` only console.log placeholder strings, with all real behavior in the postinstall dropper. Any Windows host running `npm install mailconfirmer` executes attacker-controlled code with the user's privileges.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
